title: [Performance] Order book uses O(n log n) sort on every insert
body:
## Problem

`AddOrder()` appends to the slice and then calls `sort.SliceStable()` on the entire slice. This is O(n log n) per insertion, which degrades significantly as the book grows.

For a book with 10,000 resting orders, every new order triggers a full re-sort of 10,000+ elements.

Similarly, `RemoveOrder()` does a linear scan O(n) to find the order by ID.

## Affected Files
- `pkg/orderbook/orderbook.go` — `AddOrder()`, `RemoveOrder()`

## Proposed Fix

### Short term: Binary search insertion
Use `sort.Search()` to find the insertion point and insert in-place. This reduces insertion to O(n) (due to slice shifting) but avoids the full re-sort.

### Long term: Tree-based structure
Replace sorted slices with a price-level map + sorted price tree:
```
map[float64]*PriceLevel  // price -> list of orders at that price
+ sorted tree of prices   // red-black tree or skip list
```

This gives:
- Insert: O(log p) where p = number of distinct price levels
- Remove by ID: O(1) with an order ID index
- Best price: O(1) or O(log p)

## Priority
Medium — correctness is fine, but performance limits scalability.
